title: marsilea farinosa subsp. farinosa
intro: Marsilea farinosa subsp. farinosa, also known as the Four-leaf Clover or Water Clover, is a perennial aquatic plant that belongs to the Marsileaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Marsilea farinosa subsp. farinosa include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support kidney function. The plant can be prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to support overall health and well-being.
